concept.title: Project Health Scoring
concept.shortdesc: Portfolio Manager calculates a weekly health score for each active project using a weighted combination of session completion and milestone progress. The score drives the traffic-light status indicator on the Dashboard.

section.title: Score Calculation

image.alt: Diagram showing the scoring formula: 60% session score plus 40% milestone score equals total project score
Scoring model: session score (60 points max) plus milestone score (40 points max) produces a total score from 0 to 100.

section.p: Each project receives a score from 0 to 100 for the current week. The score is the sum of two components:

dlentry.dd: (sessions with Done status ÷ sessions with Planned, Doing, or Done status) × 60 If a project has no sessions planned for the week, this component scores 0.
(milestones with Done status ÷ total milestones) × 40 If a project has no milestones defined, this component scores 0.

section.title: Traffic-Light Status
section.p: Portfolio Manager converts each score to a color status for the Dashboard:

strow.stentry: Project is progressing well this week.
Project is moving, but attention is needed.
Project has stalled or is significantly behind this week.

section.title: Score Examples
section.p: Example 1 — Green project: A novel draft project has four planned sessions and three completed (session component: 45), plus six milestones of which three are done (milestone component: 20). Total score: 65. Status: Yellow.

Example 2 — Red project: A recording project has two planned sessions and zero completed (session component: 0), plus three milestones with zero done (milestone component: 0). Total score: 0. Status: Red.

Example 3 — Sessions only: A professional-development project has no milestones defined. It has three planned sessions and three completed (session component: 60). Milestone component: 0. Total score: 60. Status: Yellow.
section.note: To reach Green status reliably, complete most of your planned sessions and advance at least some milestones each week.

section.title: Manual Score Override
section.p: You can override a project's calculated score for any week. An override requires a reason, which is stored alongside the score for future reference. Use overrides sparingly—for situations where the algorithm does not reflect the actual project state, such as a week of unrecorded off-tool work.

section.title: Portfolio Score
section.p: The Dashboard summary row displays an aggregate portfolio score, which is the average of all active projects' individual scores. The portfolio status badge reflects this average.
